zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret of England Duchess of Norfolk於1320年出生在英國素福爾可福拉苗林加姆 卡素時，她的父親Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk20歲，母親Alice de Hales19歲。她於1335年與John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年3月24日在英國倫敦享年79歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret of England Duchess of Norfolk于1320年出生在英国素福爾可福拉苗林加姆 卡素时，她的父亲Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk20岁，母亲Alice de Hales19岁。她于1335年与John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1399年3月24日在英国倫敦享年79岁，埋葬在英国英格兰波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret of England Duchess of Norfolk was born in 1320, in Framlingham Castle, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk, was 20 and her mother, Alice de Hales, was 19. She married John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave in 1335, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 24 March 1399, in London, England, at the age of 79, and was buried in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret of England Duchess of Norfolk est née en 1320, à Framlingham Castle, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alice de Hales, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave en 1335, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 24 mars 1399, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1335 in England John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 24. März 1399 im Alter von 79 Jahren in London, England und wurde in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave nel 1335, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. ko.Grey Friars: 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 리딩 그리프리아르스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret of England Duchess of Norfolk(이)가 1320년 영국 수폴크 프램링함 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Hales(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1335년 영국에서 John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 3월 24일 영국 런던에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 리딩 그리프리아르스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave em 1335, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.Grey Friars: грифрярс чурч, ридинг, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret of England Duchess of Norfolk родилась в 1320 г. в местности фрамлингхам кастл, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas of Brotherton 1st Earl of Norfolk было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Hales, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Segrave 4th Baron Segrave в 1335 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 24 марта 1399 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности грифрярс чурч, ридинг, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
